tools.name: accesschk
tools.summary: Sysinternals permission inspection utility for checking effective access on files, registry keys, services, processes, and other Windows objects.

parameters.name: [username]
parameters.description: Optional user or group to evaluate.

parameters.name: <target>
parameters.description: File, directory, registry key, service, process, object, or other supported target.

reference_items.name: -s
reference_items.description: Recurse through subkeys or subdirectories.
reference_items.use_case: Check broad writable paths or weak permission inheritance quickly.

reference_items.name: -w
reference_items.description: Show objects with write access.
reference_items.use_case: Spot privilege escalation paths faster.

reference_items.name: -u
reference_items.description: Suppress errors and show only objects with access.
reference_items.use_case: Reduce noise when triaging many candidate targets.

reference_items.name: -k
reference_items.description: Check registry keys.
reference_items.use_case: Inspect autoruns, service keys, and other registry-backed escalation paths.

reference_items.name: -c
reference_items.description: Check services.
reference_items.use_case: Review service permissions and service control opportunities.

reference_items.name: -p
reference_items.description: Check processes.
reference_items.use_case: Inspect process handles or object access in post-compromise triage.

notes: AccessChk is most useful when you already have a hypothesis about a writable path, service, or registry location and want to prove it quickly.
Save the exact command and target path when permissions become part of an escalation finding.

tools.name: Amass
tools.summary: Attack surface intelligence framework for turning scoped domains, data sources, and optional active validation into a fuller subdomain and infrastructure map.

parameters.name: <DOMAIN>
parameters.description: Registered domain in scope for enumeration.

parameters.name: -config <FILE>
parameters.description: Optional config file that keeps scope, data sources, and output settings reproducible.

reference_items.name: enum
reference_items.description: Run the main enumeration workflow.
reference_items.use_case: Build the first-pass asset map for a domain or target list.

reference_items.name: -d
reference_items.description: Set one in-scope domain directly.
reference_items.use_case: Quick focused enumeration against a single target domain.

reference_items.name: -active
reference_items.description: Enable active validation in addition to passive collection.
reference_items.use_case: Confirm findings once the engagement allows active probing.

reference_items.name: -brute
reference_items.description: Enable brute-force subdomain expansion.
reference_items.use_case: Push deeper when passive sources alone are not enough.

reference_items.name: -oA
reference_items.description: Write combined output artifacts with one basename.
reference_items.use_case: Keep enumeration output and reruns organized.

reference_items.name: datasources.yaml
reference_items.description: Configure external sources and credentials.
reference_items.use_case: Improve depth and explain where discoveries came from.

notes: Amass gets much better when the data sources are configured intentionally instead of relying on defaults alone.
Treat active mode as a scoped escalation step so the collection path stays explainable.

tools.name: bloodhound-python
tools.summary: Collector for AD relationship data that feeds BloodHound-style graph analysis without relying on the GUI first.

parameters.name: <user>
parameters.description: Directory username for collection.

parameters.name: <password>
parameters.description: Password for the collecting account.

parameters.name: <DOMAIN>
parameters.description: AD domain name such as corp.local.

reference_items.name: -u
reference_items.description: Set the username.
reference_items.use_case: Authenticate collection against the domain.

reference_items.name: -p
reference_items.description: Set the password.
reference_items.use_case: Supply credentials for collection.

reference_items.name: -d
reference_items.description: Set the AD domain.
reference_items.use_case: Keep the collection anchored to the right directory.

reference_items.name: -c
reference_items.description: Choose collection methods such as All.
reference_items.use_case: Scope how much data to gather for path analysis.

reference_items.name: -ns
reference_items.description: Specify the domain controller or DNS server.
reference_items.use_case: Control where lookups and collection traffic go.

tools.name: Browser DevTools
tools.summary: Built-in browser tooling for tracing requests, client-side state, storage, and DOM behavior before and after proxy work.

parameters.name: Open page
parameters.description: The route or app state you want to inspect live in the browser.

reference_items.name: Network
reference_items.description: Shows document, XHR, fetch, and static asset requests with timings, headers, and responses.
reference_items.use_case: Build the request map before moving to Burp or CLI replay.

reference_items.name: Application
reference_items.description: Exposes cookies, local storage, session storage, indexed DB, and service workers.
reference_items.use_case: Review auth state, tokens, persistence, and client-side trust.

reference_items.name: Sources
reference_items.description: Lets you inspect loaded scripts, source maps, breakpoints, and front-end routing logic.
reference_items.use_case: Trace hidden endpoints, validation logic, and client-side sinks.

reference_items.name: Console
reference_items.description: Runs quick JavaScript expressions and surfaces client-side errors or warnings.
reference_items.use_case: Validate assumptions without leaving the browser context.

notes: Use DevTools early when the app relies on client-side rendering, storage, or JavaScript-driven routing.

tools.name: Burp Suite
tools.summary: Intercepting proxy and manual testing suite for replaying requests, comparing responses, and validating behavior before broader automation.
tools.syntax: Burp Suite desktop -> Proxy / HTTP history / Repeater / Intruder

parameters.name: Target scope
parameters.description: The hosts, routes, or features you want Burp to collect and compare.

parameters.name: Captured request
parameters.description: A real request from the browser or client that you can replay from history or Repeater.

reference_items.name: HTTP history
reference_items.description: Keeps the exact requests and responses that actually reached the application.
reference_items.use_case: Use it as the source of truth before exporting requests to other tools.

reference_items.name: Repeater
reference_items.description: Replays a chosen request while you change one input at a time.
reference_items.use_case: Ideal for auth checks, injections, upload validation, and response diffing.

reference_items.name: Proxy Intercept
reference_items.description: Pauses traffic long enough to inspect or modify it before forwarding.
reference_items.use_case: Capture exact login, API, upload, and session requests.

reference_items.name: Logger and notes
reference_items.description: Helps you preserve useful request shapes and evidence as you work.
reference_items.use_case: Turn manual traffic into reusable CLI requests and clean writeups.

notes: Start with Burp when the request format, cookies, CSRF handling, or hidden fields are still unclear.

tools.name: Certipy
tools.summary: AD CS enumeration and abuse toolkit for mapping certificate authorities, vulnerable templates, certificate-backed auth, shadow credentials, relay, and post-exploitation certificate paths.

parameters.name: <command>
parameters.description: Action such as find, req, auth, relay, shadow, template, ca, forge, or cert.

parameters.name: -u / -p or -k
parameters.description: Credential path for LDAP and certificate service interaction.

reference_items.name: find
reference_items.description: Enumerate AD CS templates, authorities, and vulnerable ESC paths.
reference_items.use_case: Build the PKI picture before requesting or relaying anything.

reference_items.name: req
reference_items.description: Request a certificate from a chosen template.
reference_items.use_case: Test whether template controls permit alternate identities or privileged enrollment.

reference_items.name: auth
reference_items.description: Authenticate with a certificate and retrieve Kerberos material or LDAP access.
reference_items.use_case: Turn a PFX into usable identity context.

reference_items.name: relay
reference_items.description: Relay NTLM to AD CS HTTP enrollment endpoints.
reference_items.use_case: Bridge relay paths into certificate issuance when the environment allows it.

reference_items.name: shadow
reference_items.description: Work with shadow credentials.
reference_items.use_case: Establish or validate key-credential abuse paths.

reference_items.name: forge
reference_items.description: Create Golden Certificates with CA material.
reference_items.use_case: Handle high-impact post-compromise certificate abuse explicitly.

notes: Certipy is broader than simple certificate requests, so keep the AD CS role, template state, and intended abuse path documented separately.
Its outputs can include text, JSON, and BloodHound data, which is useful if you want PKI findings to sit next to the wider AD graph.

tools.name: CeWL
tools.summary: Targeted custom wordlist generator that spiders a site and turns site language, branding, and metadata into better cracking candidates.

parameters.name: <URL>
parameters.description: The start URL you want CeWL to spider.

reference_items.name: -d
reference_items.description: Set crawl depth.
reference_items.use_case: Control how far CeWL follows links from the starting page.

reference_items.name: -m
reference_items.description: Set minimum word length.
reference_items.use_case: Strip out very short noise before feeding the output into crackers.

reference_items.name: -w
reference_items.description: Write the collected words to a file.
reference_items.use_case: Create a reusable custom list for John, hashcat, or spray prep.

reference_items.name: -c
reference_items.description: Show counts for discovered words.
reference_items.use_case: Prioritize repeated organization-specific terms.

reference_items.name: -e
reference_items.description: Collect email addresses.
reference_items.use_case: Useful for username generation and identity context alongside the wordlist.

reference_items.name: --auth_type
reference_items.description: Use basic or digest authentication.
reference_items.use_case: Spider authenticated areas when the engagement requires it.

notes: CeWL defaults to staying on-site and a crawl depth of 2, which is usually safer than letting it drift widely.
A short, explainable org-specific list often beats a giant generic list when you are auditing real user behavior.

tools.name: chisel
tools.summary: Tunneling tool for reverse port forwards and SOCKS-style pivots when SSH is not the right fit.
tools.syntax: chisel server [options] or chisel client <server:port> <tunnel spec>

parameters.name: <server:port>
parameters.description: Chisel server address and port.

parameters.name: <tunnel spec>
parameters.description: Forwarding definition such as R:socks or a specific port mapping.

reference_items.name: server
reference_items.description: Run the chisel server component.
reference_items.use_case: Start the listening side of the tunnel.

reference_items.name: client
reference_items.description: Run the chisel client component.
reference_items.use_case: Connect back to the server and establish the tunnel.

reference_items.name: --reverse
reference_items.description: Allow reverse port forwarding from the client back through the server.
reference_items.use_case: Useful when the target can initiate outbound connections.

reference_items.name: R:socks
reference_items.description: Reverse SOCKS tunnel definition.
reference_items.use_case: Expose a proxy back through the connected host.

tools.name: Clairvoyance
tools.summary: GraphQL schema recovery tool that reconstructs all or part of a schema from suggestion behavior when introspection is disabled.

parameters.name: <GRAPHQL_URL>
parameters.description: Target GraphQL endpoint.

parameters.name: -o <SCHEMA_JSON>
parameters.description: Path for the recovered schema JSON output.

reference_items.name: -o
reference_items.description: Write recovered schema JSON to a file.
reference_items.use_case: Feed the schema into visualizers or other GraphQL tooling.

reference_items.name: wordlists
reference_items.description: Use general or target-specific wordlists to improve name discovery.
reference_items.use_case: Recover deeper schema detail when the default pass is too shallow.

reference_items.name: schema recovery
reference_items.description: Reconstruct schema information even with introspection disabled.
reference_items.use_case: Keep GraphQL testing moving when direct introspection is blocked.

reference_items.name: JSON output
reference_items.description: Produce schema data suitable for tools like Voyager or InQL.
reference_items.use_case: Turn raw recovery into something you can navigate and attack.

notes: Clairvoyance shines when GraphQL suggestions are still enabled, so confirm the endpoint leaks useful suggestion data before deeper runs.
Target-specific words from observed traffic or static assets often improve recovery quality significantly.

tools.name: Coercer
tools.summary: RPC coercion toolkit for scanning or triggering Windows authentication paths across multiple protocols, methods, and named pipes from one operator workflow.

parameters.name: <mode>
parameters.description: Operating mode such as scan, coerce, or fuzz depending on whether you are assessing, triggering, or researching coercion paths.

parameters.name: --target / --targets-file
parameters.description: Single target or file of targets you want to assess or coerce.

reference_items.name: scan
reference_items.description: Assess a host for reachable coercion paths and export the results.
reference_items.use_case: Host triage before you choose an exploit path.

reference_items.name: coerce
reference_items.description: Trigger authentication to the listener you specify.
reference_items.use_case: Focused relay or capture workflows.

reference_items.name: --listener-ip
reference_items.description: Set the listener host that should receive the coerced authentication.
reference_items.use_case: Tie a coercion attempt to your relay or capture box.

reference_items.name: --filter-method-name
reference_items.description: Limit testing to one coercion method by name.
reference_items.use_case: Repeatable proofs and lower-noise validation.

reference_items.name: --filter-protocol-name
reference_items.description: Restrict the scan or coercion run to one protocol family.
reference_items.use_case: Trim broad RPC coverage to the area you care about.

reference_items.name: --export-json / --export-xlsx / --export-sqlite
reference_items.description: Export scan findings for review and reporting.
reference_items.use_case: Keep the coercion surface reviewable instead of ephemeral.

notes: Coercer is best when you treat scan mode as reconnaissance and only move into coerce mode for paths you can explain and monitor.
Write down the exact method, protocol, listener, and authentication outcome so your notes survive beyond the first successful relay.

tools.name: commix
tools.summary: Automation framework for OS command injection testing that works best after you already understand the request shape and why the input is suspicious.

parameters.name: <URL>
parameters.description: Target URL when testing a direct request path.

parameters.name: <REQUEST_FILE>
parameters.description: Saved raw request used when replaying complex traffic.

reference_items.name: --url
reference_items.description: Set the target URL.
reference_items.use_case: Direct GET-style validation on a known suspect parameter.

reference_items.name: -r
reference_items.description: Load a raw request from file.
reference_items.use_case: Replay authenticated or non-trivial requests cleanly.

reference_items.name: --data
reference_items.description: Send POST data.
reference_items.use_case: Body-based injection testing when the input is not in the query string.

reference_items.name: --batch
reference_items.description: Run non-interactively.
reference_items.use_case: Automated, logged workflows after the request is understood.

reference_items.name: --tamper
reference_items.description: Use tamper scripts on injection data.
reference_items.use_case: Filter-aware validation once you know what the application is normalizing.

reference_items.name: --crawl
reference_items.description: Crawl from the target URL.
reference_items.use_case: Discover additional pages carefully when the surface is still incomplete.

notes: Commix should accelerate a validated command-injection hypothesis, not replace the manual work of building one.
Saved requests are often the cleanest way to preserve cookies, headers, and content types when the request shape matters.

tools.name: curl
tools.summary: Reliable HTTP request workbench for replaying exact traffic, measuring response changes, handling cookies, testing virtual hosts, and moving from browser/proxy evidence to reproducible terminal commands.

parameters.name: <URL>
parameters.description: The full target URL you want to request, including scheme and path.

parameters.name: <TARGET>
parameters.description: Target host, IP, URL, or service endpoint used by saved curl examples.

parameters.name: TOKEN
parameters.description: Bearer token, API key, or other authorization value copied from an in-scope session.

parameters.name: cookies.txt
parameters.description: Cookie jar file used with -b and -c to preserve state between requests.

parameters.name: headers.txt / body.html
parameters.description: Evidence files for separated response headers and response body output.

reference_items.name: -sS
reference_items.description: Run quietly while still showing errors.
reference_items.use_case: Cleaner terminal output without hiding connection or TLS failures.

reference_items.name: -i
reference_items.description: Include response headers with the body.
reference_items.use_case: Inspect cookies, redirects, cache headers, and body content together.

reference_items.name: -I
reference_items.description: Fetch response headers only.
reference_items.use_case: Quick status, redirect, and server-header checks.

reference_items.name: -k
reference_items.description: Allow insecure TLS connections.
reference_items.use_case: Use only when a lab, staging, or appliance certificate is expected to be untrusted.

reference_items.name: -L
reference_items.description: Follow redirects.
reference_items.use_case: Map login flows, canonical host redirects, and download links.

reference_items.name: -H
reference_items.description: Add a custom header to the request.
reference_items.use_case: Replay auth tokens, Host headers, content types, and client hints.

reference_items.name: --data-raw
reference_items.description: Send an exact request body without @ file expansion.
reference_items.use_case: Replay form bodies and payloads copied from Burp or DevTools.

reference_items.name: --json
reference_items.description: Send JSON and set JSON content headers automatically on modern curl versions.
reference_items.use_case: Quick API checks when you do not need to preserve a raw captured request.

reference_items.name: -b / -c
reference_items.description: Read cookies from a jar and write cookies to a jar.
reference_items.use_case: Keep login or redirect workflows reproducible across multiple requests.

reference_items.name: -F
reference_items.description: Send multipart form data.
reference_items.use_case: Upload testing and file handling validation.

reference_items.name: -o / -O
reference_items.description: Write the response to a chosen file or the remote filename.
reference_items.use_case: Save evidence, bodies, downloads, and staged files predictably.

reference_items.name: -w
reference_items.description: Print selected response metrics after the request.
reference_items.use_case: Compare status, size, timing, redirect targets, and final URLs.

reference_items.name: --resolve
reference_items.description: Pin a hostname and port to a specific IP for this request.
reference_items.use_case: Test virtual hosts and SNI routing without editing system resolver files.

reference_items.name: --path-as-is
reference_items.description: Send the URL path without dot-segment normalization.
reference_items.use_case: Traversal and path parsing tests where normalization would hide the probe.

reference_items.name: -f
reference_items.description: Fail on HTTP error status codes for transfer workflows.
reference_items.use_case: Make downloads and scripted fetches stop cleanly when the server returns an error.

notes: Prefer -sS over plain -s so curl stays quiet without hiding errors.
Use -k deliberately: an unexpected certificate failure is often useful signal, not just annoyance.
Capture a baseline with -w, -D, or -o before changing payloads so behavior changes are measurable.
Build the exact request in Burp or DevTools first, then trim the curl command to the smallest reproducible proof.

tools.name: Dalfox
tools.summary: Purpose-built XSS automation tool for replaying focused reflected or stored XSS checks once the sink and request shape are already grounded.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Input mode such as url, file, or pipe.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Target URL, file, or stdin pipeline depending on the mode.

reference_items.name: url
reference_items.description: Test one URL directly.
reference_items.use_case: Focused validation against a single suspect parameter.

reference_items.name: file
reference_items.description: Load targets from a file.
reference_items.use_case: Replay a curated list of known-meaningful URLs.

reference_items.name: pipe
reference_items.description: Read targets from stdin.
reference_items.use_case: Fit Dalfox into a tuned pipeline.

reference_items.name: --blind
reference_items.description: Configure a blind callback endpoint.
reference_items.use_case: Stored or blind XSS checks after you know the sink path.

reference_items.name: --cookie
reference_items.description: Replay cookie state.
reference_items.use_case: Authenticated XSS validation.

reference_items.name: --skip-bav
reference_items.description: Skip built-in basic vulnerability checks.
reference_items.use_case: Reduce noise when the sink is already understood.

notes: Dalfox is strongest when the target set is already curated and the app state is reproducible.
Blind or stored XSS work only stays explainable if you tie the callback and sink path back to the exact request flow.

tools.name: dirsearch
tools.summary: Directory and file brute-forcing helper for targeted path discovery once you already understand response baselines and target behavior.

parameters.name: <URL>
parameters.description: Target URL to brute force.

reference_items.name: -u
reference_items.description: Set the target URL.
reference_items.use_case: Point dirsearch at the exact base path you want to fuzz.

reference_items.name: -e
reference_items.description: Set file extensions.
reference_items.use_case: Tune the search toward the technology you already identified.

reference_items.name: -w
reference_items.description: Choose the wordlist.
reference_items.use_case: Control breadth and relevance of the brute-force path set.

reference_items.name: -x
reference_items.description: Exclude status codes.
reference_items.use_case: Filter out known noise while keeping meaningful deltas.

notes: Path brute forcing gets better after you know the framework, response baseline, and normal redirect or error pattern.
Save the exclusions and extension set you used so you can replay the exact search later.

tools.name: dnstool.py
tools.summary: krbrelayx helper for querying and modifying Active Directory-integrated DNS records over LDAP when name resolution itself becomes part of the attack path.

parameters.name: <HOSTNAME>
parameters.description: Hostname, IP, or ldap:// connection string for the LDAP/DNS target.

parameters.name: -r <RECORD>
parameters.description: FQDN of the ADIDNS record you want to query or modify.

reference_items.name: -a, --action
reference_items.description: Choose add, modify, query, remove, resurrect, or ldapdelete.
reference_items.use_case: Make record intent explicit before touching ADIDNS.

reference_items.name: -d, --data
reference_items.description: Record data such as the A-record IP.
reference_items.use_case: Point a hostname at the IP that supports your relay or operator path.

reference_items.name: --zone
reference_items.description: Set a different DNS zone manually.
reference_items.use_case: Target the right partition when the default domain zone is not enough.

reference_items.name: --forest / --legacy
reference_items.description: Switch to ForestDnsZones or legacy System partition storage.
reference_items.use_case: Handle environments that do not store the record where you first expect.

reference_items.name: -k, --kerberos
reference_items.description: Use Kerberos authentication.
reference_items.use_case: Keep auth aligned with the rest of a ticket-based workflow.

reference_items.name: --print-zones / --print-zones-dn
reference_items.description: List known zones instead of modifying records.
reference_items.use_case: Discover where a record should live before you add or change it.

notes: Treat ADIDNS changes as stateful infrastructure changes, not just another quick relay helper.
Always query the existing record or zone first so cleanup and attribution stay manageable.

tools.name: dnsx
tools.summary: Fast DNS probing and brute-force helper for resolving hostnames, validating record data, and filtering noisy DNS results before deeper scanning.

parameters.name: -l <HOSTS_FILE>
parameters.description: Resolve a host list from a file or stdin.

parameters.name: -d <DOMAIN>
parameters.description: Base domain to brute-force when paired with a wordlist.

reference_items.name: -l, -list
reference_items.description: Load subdomains or hosts from a file or stdin.
reference_items.use_case: Validate recon output before handing it to HTTP or port scanners.

reference_items.name: -d, -domain
reference_items.description: Set the domain to brute-force.
reference_items.use_case: Focused DNS expansion against one organization or zone.

reference_items.name: -w, -wordlist
reference_items.description: Use a wordlist for brute-force resolution.
reference_items.use_case: Generate candidate names once the target naming pattern is clear.

reference_items.name: -r, -resolver
reference_items.description: Provide custom resolvers.
reference_items.use_case: Stabilize results or route through trusted resolver sets.

reference_items.name: -resp
reference_items.description: Include response data in output.
reference_items.use_case: Save the exact records that justified follow-on work.

reference_items.name: -rcode
reference_items.description: Filter or inspect response codes.
reference_items.use_case: Separate real answers from NXDOMAIN noise while tuning brute-force runs.

notes: dnsx is strongest as a resolver and validator stage after subdomain discovery, not as a replacement for broader asset collection.
Save the resolver set and response output when DNS behavior itself becomes part of your evidence.

tools.name: DOM Invader
tools.summary: Burp browser tool for surfacing DOM XSS sinks, web-message abuse paths, prototype-pollution gadgets, and DOM clobbering clues during live client-side testing.

parameters.name: live browser flow
parameters.description: The authenticated or stateful app flow you want to inspect in Burp's browser.

reference_items.name: DOM XSS sink tracing
reference_items.description: Show controllable sinks and their context directly in the page tooling.
reference_items.use_case: Move from reflected client-side input to an exact sink faster.

reference_items.name: web message tooling
reference_items.description: Log, modify, and resend postMessage traffic.
reference_items.use_case: Test DOM XSS and trust mistakes in message handlers.

reference_items.name: prototype pollution analysis
reference_items.description: Identify pollution sources and controllable gadgets.
reference_items.use_case: Map client-side prototype abuse paths without manual source hunting.

reference_items.name: DOM clobbering detection
reference_items.description: Highlight potential clobbering issues.
reference_items.use_case: Spot client-side name collisions that affect security-sensitive logic.

notes: DOM Invader is built into Burp's browser, so it is most useful when you drive the real application state you care about through that browser.
Use its sink context and stack traces to bridge client-side signals back to a reproducible exploit path.

tools.name: Droopescan
tools.summary: CMS-focused scanner for identifying Drupal, WordPress, or SilverStripe installs and enumerating versions, plugins, themes, and interesting files with a controlled request profile.

parameters.name: [cms]
parameters.description: Optional CMS selector such as drupal, wordpress, or silverstripe.

parameters.name: -u <URL>
parameters.description: Target URL to scan.

reference_items.name: scan
reference_items.description: Run the main scanning workflow.
reference_items.use_case: Kick off CMS identification or a focused scan against a known platform.

reference_items.name: -u, --url
reference_items.description: Set one target URL directly.
reference_items.use_case: Validate a single CMS host cleanly.

reference_items.name: -U, --url-file
reference_items.description: Load multiple URLs from a file.
reference_items.use_case: Batch scan a curated list of candidate CMS hosts.

reference_items.name: -e, --enumerate
reference_items.description: Choose checks like plugins, themes, versions, or interesting URLs.
reference_items.use_case: Reduce noise and match the scan to the question you actually need answered.

reference_items.name: --number / --threads
reference_items.description: Control request volume and concurrency.
reference_items.use_case: Tune scan speed without overloading the target.

reference_items.name: json output
reference_items.description: Support machine-friendly output in addition to standard console output.
reference_items.use_case: Feed results into reporting or comparison workflows.

notes: The project README explicitly warns that version findings still need manual correlation to real vulnerabilities rather than automatic trust.
Droopescan also documents support for .netrc files and proxy environment variables, which makes it easier to fit into authenticated or intercepted testing.

tools.name: enum4linux-ng
tools.summary: Aggregated SMB, RPC, and domain enumeration helper that is useful for getting a quick multi-protocol baseline on Windows targets.

parameters.name: <TARGET>
parameters.description: Target Windows or Samba host.

reference_items.name: -A
reference_items.description: Run a broad enumeration pass.
reference_items.use_case: Initial baseline when you want a fast multi-check snapshot.

reference_items.name: -As
reference_items.description: Run a broad pass with share enumeration.
reference_items.use_case: Add share visibility to the baseline.

reference_items.name: -u
reference_items.description: Authenticate with a username.
reference_items.use_case: Post-auth enumeration when null sessions are not enough.

reference_items.name: -p
reference_items.description: Supply a password.
reference_items.use_case: Authenticated SMB/RPC checks.

notes: This is good for triage, but the real value is following up the interesting result with the protocol-native tool that proved it.
Capture which parts of the aggregate output actually changed your next step.

tools.name: Feroxbuster
tools.summary: Recursive content discovery tool that is especially useful when you want fast route expansion with flexible wordlists and extensions.

reference_items.name: /cert:ignore
reference_items.description: Ignore certificate validation errors.
reference_items.use_case: Lab or internal cases where the target certificate is self-signed and you already understand the trust tradeoff.

reference_items.name: +clipboard
reference_items.description: Enable clipboard redirection.
reference_items.use_case: Controlled operator workflows that need copy/paste between systems.

reference_items.name: /dynamic-resolution
reference_items.description: Resize the session dynamically.
reference_items.use_case: Make the session easier to work with from different client window sizes.

reference_items.name: /f
reference_items.description: Use fullscreen mode.
reference_items.use_case: Longer interactive sessions where window management matters.

notes: The FreeRDP user manual recommends separate /u, /d, and /p values because backslashes in domain-qualified usernames are easy to mis-handle in shells.
Certificate handling and redirection settings should match the reason you are connecting, not just habit.
